trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: White House journalists, Google/physicists collabs
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 03/16/26

urls.page_title: World According to Sound offers immersive audio experience March 23
urls.page_summary: A sonic experience where the audience sits blindfolded is returning to Cornell March 23 for a 6 p.m. performance in Sage Chapel.

The College of Arts & Sciences’ Arts Unplugged series brings research and creative works into the public sphere for discussion and inspiration. These engaging events invite a broad audience to explore the work of scholars, faculty and creative lum...
urls.page_title: Stem-cell registry drive will mobilize campus to save lives | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: To help the son of an alum and thousands of people in need of life-saving intervention, Cornell is hosting a stem-cell cheek swabbing campaign March 13-20 across the Ithaca campus.
urls.page_title: Inside Journalism: A Conversation with NPR’s Robin Young and the NY Times’ Zolan Kanno-Youngs - Cornell
urls.page_summary: "Inside Journalism: A Conversation with NPR’s Robin Young and the NY Times’ Zolan Kanno-Youngs" will be moderated by Sophia Dasser ’28, Editor-in-Chief of the Cornell Daily Sun. The conversation will explore careers in journalism today in both pri...
urls.page_title: Renowned pianist Sir Stephen Hough plays Dallas Morse Coors Concert Series
urls.page_summary: In the concert, Hough will play an imaginative program that includes works by Schubert, Brahms, Schoenberg, Beethoven and a suite from "Mary Poppins."
urls.page_title: NY Times writer and Klarman '79: Civil debate can change minds
urls.page_summary: A live and online audience of nearly 1,000 tapped into an ongoing conversation between Bret Stephens and Seth Klarman about media, democracy, education and the nature of debate.
urls.page_title: PENUMBRA: 2026 ANNUAL SPRING DANCE PRESENTING SERIES - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Join the Department of Performing and Media Arts for PENUMBRA: 2026 ANNUAL SPRING DANCE PRESENTING SERIES, an evening of original dance work featuring choreography by Babatunji Johnson and Assistant Professor of the Practice Danielle Russo, on Fri...
urls.page_title: Testing large language models on scientific literature | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Cornell physicists and Google researchers engaged a panel of 12 human experts to test the ability of six LLM systems to understand scientific literature at the level of a specialist.
urls.page_title: Professional Directions: A Conversation with Keri Putnam, A.D. White Professor-at-Large - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Join the Department of Performing and Media Arts for Professional Directions: A Conversation with Keri Putnam, A.D. White Professor-at-Large on Thursday, March 19, at 2:00 pm, in the Film Forum, Schwartz Center for the Performing Arts. Free and op...
